message:
fix(cli): address code review feedback for backend/sync mode commands

- Change backend command group from "sync" to "setup" (more appropriate)
- Add "current" to readOnlyCommands map for bd sync mode current
- Remove redundant "message" field from JSON output, add description instead

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Similar to 'git config --list' or 'npm config list' patterns for discovering valid values.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"beads/crew/wolf","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T23:24:17Z","created_by":"gastown/crew/george","updated_at":"2026-01-16T23:35:25Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T23:35:25Z","labels":["desire-path"],"work_type":"mutex"}
 {"id":"bd-9ji4z","title":"Trust child repos during multi-repo hydration (replaces PR #971)","description":"## Problem

PR #971 (https://github.com/steveyegge/beads/pull/971) tries to solve a real problem: when hydrating issues from child repos, custom types defined in the child fail validation because the parent doesn't know about them.

Example:
- Child repo `oss/` has `types.custom = [bd, pm, llm]`
- Parent hydrates issue `oss-abc` with type `pm`
- Parent's config doesn't have `pm` → validation fails

PR #971's solution: Add `custom_types` to `repos.additional` config so parent explicitly tracks child types.

## Why PR #971 Is Wrong

It violates **Discover, Don't Track** (PRIMING.md):

> Don't maintain separate tracking for what's observable. The beads ARE the source of truth.

The child repo already knows its own types. Duplicating them in parent config is:
- Redundant state that can drift
- Manual sync burden when child adds types  
- Anti-pattern in Gas Town's federation model

## The Right Solution: Trust + Discover

### Trust (Primary)

From CONTEXT.md federation model: each chain validates its own work. The child repo already validated these issues when they were created. The JSONL is a "signed export" from a working Beads database.

**Don't re-validate custom types during import. Trust the source.**

- Built-in types (task, bug, feature, epic, chore): validate normally
- Custom types: skip validation - child already validated them

### Discover (When Needed)

If we ever need to know child's types (diagnostics, UI, routing):
- Read from child's config/DB on demand
- Never cache in parent config
- `bd doctor` can discover and report mismatches as warnings

## Implementation

### Phase 1: Trust during import

Modify `upsertIssueInTx` in `internal/storage/sqlite/multirepo.go`:

```go
// Add to types/types.go
func (t IssueType) IsBuiltIn() bool {
    switch t {
    case TypeTask, TypeBug, TypeFeature, TypeEpic, TypeChore:
        return true
    }
    return false
}

// In ValidateWithCustom or upsertIssueInTx:
// For multi-repo import, only validate built-in types
// Custom types are trusted from source repo
```

### Phase 2: Discovery for diagnostics (optional)

Add `bd doctor` check that:
1. Reads each child repo's config/DB
2. Discovers their custom types
3. Reports any issues with unknown types as warnings (not errors)

## Benefits

- Simpler than PR #971 (removes 1200 lines of config plumbing)
- No new config format needed
- Backwards compatible
- Aligned with Gas Town federation model
- Child repos can evolve freely without updating parent

## Test Plan

- [ ] Hydrate child with custom type not in parent → succeeds
- [ ] Hydrate child with invalid built-in type → fails (still validated)
- [ ] Existing single-repo validation unchanged
- [ ] `bd doctor` discovers and reports child types

## References

- PR #971: https://github.com/steveyegge/beads/pull/971
- PRIMING.md: Discover, Don't Track principle
- CONTEXT.md: Federation model","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-09T22:05:36Z","created_by":"beads/crew/emma","updated_at":"2026-01-09T22:26:42Z","closed_at":"2026-01-09T22:26:42Z","work_type":"mutex"}
 {"id":"bd-9qajq","title":"gt sling crew auto-picks random crew worker (violates crew model)","description":"## Problem

`gt sling bd-xxx crew` auto-dispatches to a random crew worker. This violates the crew model:

- **Crew** = persistent, human-managed workspaces. Human decides who gets what work.
- **Polecats** = transient worker pool. Auto-dispatch makes sense.

Current behavior interrupts crew workers unexpectedly, which is the antithesis of what crew are for.

## Expected Behavior

Either:
1. Require explicit crew worker name: `gt sling bd-xxx crew/fang` (no auto-pick)
2. Or reject `gt sling ... crew` entirely - crew assignment should be manual

## Workaround

Assign beads manually with `bd update --assignee` and send mail.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-12T09:36:46Z","created_by":"beads/crew/emma","updated_at":"2026-01-12T09:36:46Z","work_type":"mutex"}
-{"id":"bd-9qywp","title":"Implement import processing for JSONL deletion markers","description":"The incremental export now writes deletion markers to JSONL:
```json
{"id": "gt-123", "_deleted": true, "_deleted_at": "2026-01-17T10:30:00Z"}
```

However, the import side was not updated to process these markers. When importing JSONL with deletion markers, we need to:
1. Detect entries with `_deleted: true`
2. Call `store.DeleteIssue()` for those IDs
3. Skip them from normal issue creation/update

This is necessary for proper bidirectional sync with deletion support.

Files to modify:
- cmd/bd/import.go
- internal/importer/importer.go
- Possibly internal/storage/jsonl/ if there's JSONL parsing there","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-21T03:08:20Z","created_by":"beads/crew/darcy","updated_at":"2026-01-21T03:08:20Z","work_type":"mutex"}
+{"id":"bd-9qywp","title":"Implement import processing for JSONL deletion markers","description":"The incremental export now writes deletion markers to JSONL:
```json
{"id": "gt-123", "_deleted": true, "_deleted_at": "2026-01-17T10:30:00Z"}
```

However, the import side was not updated to process these markers. When importing JSONL with deletion markers, we need to:
1. Detect entries with `_deleted: true`
2. Call `store.DeleteIssue()` for those IDs
3. Skip them from normal issue creation/update

This is necessary for proper bidirectional sync with deletion support.

Files to modify:
- cmd/bd/import.go
- internal/importer/importer.go
- Possibly internal/storage/jsonl/ if there's JSONL parsing there","status":"hooked","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/crew/darcy","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-21T03:08:20Z","created_by":"beads/crew/darcy","updated_at":"2026-01-27T05:11:42Z","work_type":"mutex"}

 {"id":"bd-i3ed","title":"Refactor bd setup to recipe-based architecture (GH#898)","description":"## Summary

Replace tool-specific setup commands (cursor, claude, etc.) with a generic recipe-based system. New tools become config entries, not code changes.

## Motivation

GH#898 requests Kilo Code integration. Current approach requires new Go code for each tool:
- `cmd/bd/setup/cursor.go`
- `cmd/bd/setup/kilocode.go` (proposed)
- `cmd/bd/setup/windsurf.go` (future?)
- ...

This does not scale. All these integrations do the same thing:
1. Generate markdown content (beads workflow instructions)
2. Write to tool-specific path

The **content** is identical. Only the **destination path** varies.

## Design

### Core Insight

Beads does not need to know anything about Kilo Code, Cursor, or Claude Code beyond where they read their rules files. That is a path mapping—data, not code.

### Recipe Format

```toml
# .beads/recipes.toml (user additions/overrides)
[cursor]
path = ".cursor/rules/bd.md"

[claude]
path = "CLAUDE.md"

[kilocode]
path = ".kilocode/rules/bd.md"

[my-custom-editor]
path = ".myeditor/config/bd.md"
```

### Built-in Defaults

Ship sensible defaults compiled into binary:
- cursor → `.cursor/rules/bd.md`
- claude → `CLAUDE.md`
- windsurf → `.windsurf/rules/bd.md`
- cody → `.cody/rules/bd.md` (verify path)

User file overrides/extends built-in defaults.

### Commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `bd setup <recipe>` | Write template to recipe path |
| `bd setup --list` | Show available recipes (built-in + user) |
| `bd setup --print` | Dump template to stdout |
| `bd setup -o <path>` | Write to arbitrary path (escape hatch) |
| `bd setup <recipe> --check` | Verify if installed |
| `bd setup <recipe> --remove` | Delete the file |
| `bd setup --add <name> <path>` | Save custom recipe |

### Template

Single markdown template embedded in binary. Contains:
- What beads is
- Key commands (`bd ready`, `bd create`, `bd close`, etc.)
- Workflow guidance
- JSON flags for programmatic use

### Implementation Plan

1. Add `--print` and `-o` flags to existing setup command
2. Create `internal/recipes/` package
   - Built-in recipes (Go map or embed)
   - Load/merge user `.beads/recipes.toml`
3. Refactor `cmd/bd/setup/setup.go` to use recipes
4. Remove tool-specific files (`cursor.go`, etc.)
5. Add `--add` command to save custom recipes
6. Update documentation

### Migration

Existing `bd setup cursor` continues to work—just backed by recipe lookup instead of dedicated code.

## Out of Scope (for now)

- `--global` flag (user-level config paths) — use `-o` escape hatch
- `mode: append` (add to existing file) — wait until requested
- GitHub recipe registry — overkill for path mappings
- TOML migration for config.yaml — separate initiative

## Response to GH#898

Once implemented, respond to PR:

> Thanks for the contribution! We are refactoring `bd setup` to a recipe-based approach where new tools are config entries, not code changes.
>
> Instead of new Go files, please submit a PR adding Kilo Code to the built-in recipes in `internal/recipes/builtin.go` (or equivalent). Happy to help if you have questions!

## References

- GH#898: Kilo Code integration request
- Current setup code: `cmd/bd/setup/`

 {"id":"bd-i54l","title":"Extract Gas Town-specific issue types from beads core","description":"dispatched_by: beads/crew/dave

## Problem

Beads has accumulated issue types that are specific to Gas Town's orchestration model:

```go
TypeAgent   IssueType = "agent"   // Agent identity bead
TypeRole    IssueType = "role"    // Agent role definition  
TypeRig     IssueType = "rig"     // Rig identity bead (project container)
TypeConvoy  IssueType = "convoy"  // Cross-project tracking
TypeSlot    IssueType = "slot"    // Exclusive access slot (merge-slot gate)
```

This violates separation of concerns. Beads is supposed to be a **generic AI-friendly issue tracker** that Gas Town happens to use - not "Gas Town's issue tracker."

## Impact

- **Credibility**: Beads can't be presented as a standalone tool when it has hardcoded knowledge of a specific orchestration system
- **Coupling**: Changes to Gas Town's identity model require beads releases
- **Confusion**: Users outside Gas Town see types that don't apply to them

## Solution

### Option A: Labels instead of types
Gas Town uses labels like `gt:agent`, `gt:role`, `gt:rig` instead of dedicated types. Beads stays generic.

### Option B: Type extension mechanism
Beads supports custom type registration via config. Gas Town registers its types at init time.

### Option C: Separate identity store
Gas Town maintains agent/role/rig identity outside beads entirely. Beads only tracks work items.

## Types to evaluate

| Type | Keep in beads? | Rationale |
|------|----------------|-----------|
| `gate` | Yes | Generic workflow concept (async waits) |
| `molecule` | Yes | Generic workflow templates |
| `agent` | **No** | Gas Town identity |
| `role` | **No** | Gas Town identity |
| `rig` | **No** | Gas Town identity |
| `convoy` | **No** | Gas Town cross-rig tracking |
| `slot` | Maybe | Could be generic "mutex" concept |

## Files affected

- `internal/types/types.go` - Type definitions
- `cmd/bd/create.go` - Type validation
- `cmd/bd/update.go` - Type validation  
- `internal/storage/sqlite/migrations/030_agent_fields.go` - Schema fields
- Possibly others with type switch statements","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"beads/crew/dave","created_at":"2026-01-07T04:26:52Z","created_by":"beads/crew/dave","updated_at":"2026-01-07T06:19:33Z","closed_at":"2026-01-07T06:19:33Z","labels":["architecture","separation-of-concerns"],"work_type":"mutex"}
 {"id":"bd-i8zab","title":"Batch wisp deletion in deleteSessionCostWisps","description":"## Problem

`deleteSessionCostWisps()` (internal/cmd/costs.go:1188) burns wisps one at a time in a loop. This creates O(n) subprocess calls for deletion.

## Location

internal/cmd/costs.go:1143-1191

## Solution

Check if `bd mol burn` supports multiple IDs. If so, collect IDs and burn in one call.
If not, consider adding batch support to bd.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/polecats/quartz","created_at":"2026-01-09T06:55:16Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-01-09T08:25:54Z","closed_at":"2026-01-09T08:25:54Z","work_type":"mutex"}

**Test file:** cmd/bd/backend_test.go and/or cmd/bd/sync_mode_cmd_test.go","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-27T05:11:57Z","created_by":"beads/crew/lydia","updated_at":"2026-01-27T05:11:57Z"}
 {"id":"bd-ihu31","title":"Add --ready flag to bd list to show only available work","description":"## Problem
`bd list` by default shows all non-closed issues including hooked ones. Hooked work is actively being worked on and shouldn't appear in the pool of available work.

## Solution
Add a `--ready` flag to `bd list` that filters to show only work that can actually be picked up:
- Status is `open` (not hooked, not in_progress, not blocked, not deferred)
- Could potentially also exclude assigned issues (optional consideration)

## Current Workaround
`bd list --status open` works but isn't as intuitive as `--ready`

## Implementation
- Add `--ready` boolean flag to list command
- When set, filter to status=open only
- Consider if it should also filter out assigned issues (probably not by default)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"beads/crew/dave","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-12T09:59:57Z","created_by":"beads/crew/dave","updated_at":"2026-01-12T10:05:30Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T10:05:30Z","work_type":"mutex"}
 {"id":"bd-ik1b","title":"Add --validate flag to bd create","description":"Opt-in strict mode that fails creation if required sections are missing. Uses ValidateTemplate() under the hood.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-01T19:19:09Z","created_by":"beads/crew/grip","updated_at":"2026-01-04T23:22:09Z","closed_at":"2026-01-01T22:20:07Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ik1b","depends_on_id":"bd-615z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T23:16:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-ik1b","depends_on_id":"bd-ou35","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-23T23:16:08Z","created_by":"import"}],"work_type":"mutex"}
